Qs.Metadata & Update Cadence:
Supply only externally checkable facts:
• Headline text
• Outlet name + publication date (ISO YYYY‑MM‑DD)
• Author(s)
• Stated update schedule (e.g., “updated weekly as new BLS data arrive”) — if none, write "One‑off publication"
• If the piece is part of an ongoing series, note the series name and link.

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “The graphic footer states ‘Last updated 2025‑05‑10’. Outlet is FiveThirtyEight; past items in the ‘Tracking Inflation’ series refreshed monthly…”
Qs.Core Question & Takeaway:
Articulate (a) the central question the article answers and (b) the single most important conclusion it offers. Keep both under 40 words each and avoid evaluative language.

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Q: ‘How fast are rents rising in major U.S. metros?’ Takeaway: ‘Rents are decelerating in Sun Belt cities but still exceeding national CPI in coastal metros.’”
Qs.Dataset Description:
Create a markdown table:
| # | Dataset Name | Source / Owner | Timeframe | Rows / Size | Public Link | License |
Include *all* datasets explicitly referenced or linked. If the article aggregates multiple public tables into one, treat that as a distinct row titled “Compiled dataset”.

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Row 1: Zillow Observed Rent Index; 2018‑01 → 2025‑03; ~1.2 M rows…”
Qs.Methodology Summary:
Bullet the key analytic steps **as described or implied**:
• Data cleaning/normalization
• Statistical tests or models (regression, smoothing, clustering…)
• Margin‑of‑error or confidence intervals
• Any imputation or weighting
Include citations to the article’s wording (“The authors used LOESS smoothing…”).

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “They ‘adjusted for seasonal effects using X‑13 ARIMA’; computed YoY percent change; no mention of heteroskedasticity checks…”
Qs.Visualization Audit:
List every major chart/graphic in a markdown table:
| # | Figure Type (bar, line, choropleth…) | Variables Shown | Potential Misleading Features (truncated axis, non‑zero baseline, color scale bias, suppressed N/A) |
If no questionable design choices, explicitly say “None detected”.

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Fig 2 line chart truncates y‑axis at 40 %, exaggerating slope…”
Qs.Code & Reproducibility:
Answer Y/N: Is analysis code publicly available? If yes, give repo/URL and note language (e.g., ‘Python notebook, R script’). If no, state “Code not published”. Mention whether raw data are downloadable or behind paywall.

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “GitHub repo linked in footer; contains Jupyter notebook with Matplotlib plots; datasets stored in /data.”
Qs.Limitations & Uncertainty:
Provide a bullet list of caveats that readers should keep in mind—draw from the article plus outside IR evidence.
• Sampling bias or missing regions
• Model assumptions
• Data lag vs. real‑time relevance
• Known disputes among experts
If the article already states limits, quote or paraphrase them; then add any additional issues surfaced via IR.

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Article admits ‘does not adjust rents for unit size’; IR found a Brookings critique on lagged Zillow coverage in rural counties…”
